From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

This article is about the metropolitan borough in the West Midlands. For the town it is named for, see [Solihull](/wiki/Solihull "Solihull").

Metropolitan borough in England

The **Metropolitan Borough of Solihull**, often colloquially just called **Solihull**,[[a]](#cite_note-2) is a metropolitan borough in the east of the [West Midlands county](/wiki/West_Midlands_(county) "West Midlands (county)"), England. It is named after its largest town, [Solihull](/wiki/Solihull "Solihull"), from which [Solihull Metropolitan Borough Council](/wiki/Solihull_Metropolitan_Borough_Council "Solihull Metropolitan Borough Council") is based. Much of the borough is considered to be part of the [West Midlands conurbation](/wiki/West_Midlands_conurbation "West Midlands conurbation"), and the borough's area was historically in Northwestern [Warwickshire](/wiki/Warwickshire "Warwickshire").[[2]](#cite_note-Warks-3) For [Eurostat](/wiki/Eurostat "Eurostat") purposes it is a NUTS 3 region (code UKG32) and is one of seven boroughs or unitary districts that comprise the "West Midlands" [NUTS 2 region](/wiki/NUTS_statistical_regions_of_the_United_Kingdom "NUTS statistical regions of the United Kingdom").

Much of the large residential population in the north of the borough centres on the communities of [Chelmsley Wood](/wiki/Chelmsley_Wood "Chelmsley Wood"), [Castle Bromwich](/wiki/Castle_Bromwich "Castle Bromwich"), [Marston Green](/wiki/Marston_Green "Marston Green") and [Smith's Wood](/wiki/Smith%27s_Wood "Smith's Wood") as well as the towns of [Kingshurst](/wiki/Kingshurst "Kingshurst") and [Fordbridge](/wiki/Fordbridge "Fordbridge").[[3]](#cite_note-4) In the south are the towns of [Shirley](/wiki/Shirley,_West_Midlands "Shirley, West Midlands") and Solihull. The borough also contains the large villages of [Knowle](/wiki/Knowle,_West_Midlands "Knowle, West Midlands"), [Dorridge](/wiki/Dorridge "Dorridge"), [Meriden](/wiki/Meriden,_West_Midlands "Meriden, West Midlands") and [Balsall Common](/wiki/Balsall_Common "Balsall Common"), all of which are outside of the West Midlands conurbation, to its east.

Since 2011, Solihull has formed part of the [Greater Birmingham & Solihull Local Enterprise Partnership](/wiki/Greater_Birmingham_%26_Solihull_Local_Enterprise_Partnership "Greater Birmingham & Solihull Local Enterprise Partnership") along with neighbouring authorities [Birmingham](/wiki/Birmingham_City_Council "Birmingham City Council"), [Bromsgrove](/wiki/Bromsgrove_District "Bromsgrove District"), [Cannock Chase](/wiki/Cannock_Chase_District "Cannock Chase District"), [East Staffordshire](/wiki/East_Staffordshire "East Staffordshire"), [Lichfield](/wiki/Lichfield_District_Council "Lichfield District Council"), [Redditch](/wiki/Redditch "Redditch"), [Tamworth](/wiki/Tamworth_(borough) "Tamworth (borough)") and [Wyre Forest](/wiki/Wyre_Forest_District "Wyre Forest District").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Metropolitan_Borough_of_Solihull&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0a/Solihull_Crest.JPG/250px-Solihull_Crest.JPG)](/wiki/File:Solihull_Crest.JPG)

The [coat of arms](/wiki/Coat_of_arms "Coat of arms") of Solihull Metropolitan Borough Council

Solihull probably derived its name from a 'miry or muddy' or soily hill. The parish church was built on a hill of stiff red [marl](/wiki/Marl "Marl"), which turned to sticky mud in wet weather.

Solihull was an [ancient parish](/wiki/Ancient_parish "Ancient parish"), covering the town itself and adjoining rural areas, including [Shirley](/wiki/Shirley,_West_Midlands "Shirley, West Midlands").[[4]](#cite_note-5) Solihull was made the centre of a [poor law union](/wiki/Poor_law_union "Poor law union") in 1836, covering eleven parishes: [Baddesley Clinton](/wiki/Baddesley_Clinton "Baddesley Clinton"), [Balsall](/wiki/Balsall "Balsall"), [Barston](/wiki/Barston "Barston"), [Elmdon](/wiki/Elmdon,_West_Midlands "Elmdon, West Midlands"), [Knowle](/wiki/Knowle,_West_Midlands "Knowle, West Midlands"), [Lapworth](/wiki/Lapworth "Lapworth"), [Nuthurst](/wiki/Hockley_Heath#Nuthurst "Hockley Heath"), [Packwood](/wiki/Packwood,_England "Packwood, England"), Solihull, [Tanworth](/wiki/Tanworth-in-Arden "Tanworth-in-Arden") and [Yardley](/wiki/Yardley,_Birmingham "Yardley, Birmingham"). Yardley was in [Worcestershire](/wiki/Worcestershire "Worcestershire") and the rest of the parishes were in [Warwickshire](/wiki/Warwickshire "Warwickshire").[[5]](#cite_note-6)

Such poor law unions formed the basis for later local government areas. In 1872 poor law unions also became [rural sanitary districts](/wiki/Rural_sanitary_district "Rural sanitary district") for the parts of their areas without urban authorities; there were no urban authorities in the Solihull union. In 1894 rural sanitary districts were converted into [rural districts](/wiki/Rural_district "Rural district") with their own elected councils under the [Local Government Act 1894](/wiki/Local_Government_Act_1894 "Local Government Act 1894"). The 1894 Act split districts which straddled county boundaries, and so Yardley became a separate rural district (which was later absorbed into [Birmingham](/wiki/Birmingham "Birmingham") in 1911), whilst the Warwickshire parishes from the Solihull poor law union became the Solihull Rural District.[[6]](#cite_note-7) The 1894 Act also created parish councils for [rural parishes](/wiki/Rural_parish "Rural parish"), including Solihull.

The Solihull Rural District and the Solihull Parish Council were abolished in 1932. A new [urban district](/wiki/Urban_district_(England_and_Wales) "Urban district (England and Wales)") of Solihull was created; the parishes of Solihull, Elmdon, Knowle, Nuthurst, Packwood and Sheldon[[b]](#cite_note-8) were abolished and most of their combined area became the new urban district, subject to various adjustments to the boundaries with neighbouring parishes.[[7]](#cite_note-9) The parts of the old Solihull Rural District not included were added to neighbouring rural districts.

Expansion continued and [Queen Elizabeth II](/wiki/Elizabeth_II_of_the_United_Kingdom "Elizabeth II of the United Kingdom") granted a charter in 1954 making Solihull into a [Municipal Borough](/wiki/Municipal_Borough "Municipal Borough"); ten years later it was given the status of [County Borough](/wiki/County_Borough "County Borough"). Reorganisation of boundaries and council responsibilities in 1974 created the Metropolitan Borough of Solihull by the merger of the [Solihull](/wiki/Solihull "Solihull") [County Borough](/wiki/County_Borough "County Borough") and most of the [Meriden Rural District](/wiki/Meriden_Rural_District "Meriden Rural District"), which forms the main rural part of the borough and county. It included Balsall Common, Barston, Berkswell, Bickenhill, Castle Bromwich, Chelmsley Wood, Elmdon, Fordbridge, Hampton in Arden, Hockley Heath, Kingshurst, Knowle, Marston Green, Meriden, Olton, Smiths Wood, Solihull, Shirley and Temple Balsall.

In 1986 the Solihull borough effectively became a [unitary authority](/wiki/Unitary_authority "Unitary authority") when the [West Midlands County Council](/wiki/West_Midlands_County_Council "West Midlands County Council") was abolished. It remains part of the West Midlands for [ceremonial purposes](/wiki/Ceremonial_counties_of_England "Ceremonial counties of England"), and for functions such as police, fire and public transport. There is some support to return the borough to Warwickshire for ceremonial purposes, as was the case when the County of Avon was abolished and Bath was returned to Somerset.[[8]](#cite_note-10) There were also complaints that Solihull was not involved in the design of the [Warwickshire flag](/wiki/Flag_of_Warwickshire "Flag of Warwickshire") in 2016.[[9]](#cite_note-11)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Metropolitan_Borough_of_Solihull&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

The borough is bordered by the [M6](/wiki/M6_motorway "M6 motorway") and the [M40](/wiki/M40_motorway "M40 motorway") and split by the [M42](/wiki/M42_motorway "M42 motorway") which divides the urban centre of the borough from the rural south and east. The borough's transport links have led to a number of established large businesses being based in the borough, such as [Land Rover](/wiki/Land_Rover "Land Rover"), the [National Exhibition Centre](/wiki/National_Exhibition_Centre "National Exhibition Centre") and [Birmingham Airport](/wiki/Birmingham_Airport "Birmingham Airport"). A short automatic light transport system links the airport to the nearby [Birmingham International railway station](/wiki/Birmingham_International_railway_station "Birmingham International railway station"). Around three-quarters of the borough is [greenbelt](/wiki/Green_belt "Green belt") and a large proportion of that is worked farmland. The borough shares its boundaries with [Birmingham](/wiki/Birmingham "Birmingham") to the west and north, [Coventry](/wiki/Coventry "Coventry") to the east, Warwickshire to both the north and south and [Worcestershire](/wiki/Worcestershire "Worcestershire") to the south west. The borough contains a sizeable rural area known as the *[Meriden Gap](/wiki/Meriden_Gap "Meriden Gap")* (after the village of [Meriden](/wiki/Meriden,_West_Midlands "Meriden, West Midlands")) which serves as a [green belt](/wiki/Green_belt "Green belt") separating the Birmingham conurbation from the city of Coventry. Parts of Solihull neighbour the suburbs of [Minworth](/wiki/Minworth "Minworth") and [Hall Green](/wiki/Hall_Green "Hall Green"). Parts also lie close to (but not contiguous with) the town of [Coleshill](/wiki/Coleshill,_Warwickshire "Coleshill, Warwickshire") and city of [Coventry](/wiki/Coventry "Coventry").

### Council

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Metropolitan_Borough_of_Solihull&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Council")]

See also: [Solihull local elections](/wiki/Solihull_local_elections "Solihull local elections")

Elections to [Solihull Metropolitan Borough Council](/wiki/Solihull_Metropolitan_Borough_Council "Solihull Metropolitan Borough Council") are held in three out of every four years, with one third of the 51 seats on the council being elected at each election. Since the [2011 election](/wiki/2011_Solihull_Council_election "2011 Solihull Council election") The Conservative Party has had a majority on the council.

At the [2024 election](/wiki/2024_Solihull_Metropolitan_Borough_Council_election "2024 Solihull Metropolitan Borough Council election") to the council, the following members were returned:

| Year | [Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)") | [Green](/wiki/Green_Party_of_England_and_Wales "Green Party of England and Wales") | [Liberal Democrat](/wiki/Liberal_Democrats_(UK) "Liberal Democrats (UK)") | [Independent](/wiki/Independent_(politician) "Independent (politician)") | [Labour](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2024 | 30 | 10 | 8 | 2 | 1 |  |

For election purposes the council is divided up into the following wards based on [civil parishes](/wiki/Civil_parishes_in_England "Civil parishes in England"). Each ward is represented by three councillors:

* [Bickenhill](/wiki/Bickenhill "Bickenhill")
* [Blythe](/wiki/River_Blythe "River Blythe")
* [Castle Bromwich](/wiki/Castle_Bromwich "Castle Bromwich")
* [Chelmsley Wood](/wiki/Chelmsley_Wood "Chelmsley Wood")
* [Dorridge](/wiki/Dorridge "Dorridge") and [Hockley Heath](/wiki/Hockley_Heath "Hockley Heath")
* [Elmdon](/wiki/Elmdon,_West_Midlands "Elmdon, West Midlands")

## Coat of arms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Metropolitan_Borough_of_Solihull&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Coat of arms")]

The constituent parts of the borough's coat of arms are:

* Battlements, sickles and an oak tree with golden acorns, which represent the rural and agricultural nature of the Forest of Arden.
* The Black Griffin is taken from the arms of the [Earls of Aylesford](/wiki/Earl_of_Aylesford "Earl of Aylesford"), who are associated with Meriden.
* The Silver Fleur-de-lys comes from the Digby family, who were associated with Fordbridge.
* The Black Greyhound is taken from the arms of the Greswolds, the family who built the 15th century house called the Manor House in the High Street, Solihull.

A stylised version of the coat of arms can be seen on the top left of Solihull Metropolitan Borough Council's website pages, and the official, heraldic version appears on a dedicated page on the same site – external links below.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Metropolitan_Borough_of_Solihull&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Economy")]

Solihull is regarded as having one of the strongest subregional economies in the West Midlands, with a significantly higher nominal GVA per capita and Silhillians enjoying considerably higher disposable income rates than both regional and UK averages.[[10]](#cite_note-12)[[11]](#cite_note-13)

Analysis also shows Solihull as having the lowest (and fastest falling) claimant count for [Jobseeker's Allowance](/wiki/Jobseeker%27s_Allowance "Jobseeker's Allowance") in the region.[[12]](#cite_note-14)

This is a chart of trend of regional gross value added of Solihull at current basic prices published (pp. 240–253) by *Office for National Statistics* with figures in millions of British Pounds Sterling.

| Year | Regional Gross Value Added[4](#endnote_4) | Agriculture[1](#endnote_1) | Industry[2](#endnote_2) | Services[3](#endnote_3) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1995 | **1,929** | 12 | 496 | 1,421 |
| 2000 | **2,959** | 8 | 870 | 2,082 |
| 2003 | **4,023** | 8 | 1,121 | 2,893 |

**[^1](#ref_1)** includes hunting and forestry

**[^2](#ref_2)** includes energy and construction

**[^3](#ref_3)** includes financial intermediation services indirectly measured

**[^4](#ref_4)** Components may not sum to totals due to rounding

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Metropolitan_Borough_of_Solihull&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Dorian Yates](/wiki/Dorian_Yates "Dorian Yates") - IFBB Pro Bodybuilder and six-time Mr Olympia winner (1992-1997) born in Solihull
* [Michael Buerk](/wiki/Michael_Buerk "Michael Buerk") – BBC news presenter and journalist born and educated in Solihull
* [Jasper Carrott](/wiki/Jasper_Carrott "Jasper Carrott") – Born and lived in the Solihull Area
* [Lucy Davis](/wiki/Lucy_Davis "Lucy Davis") - Daughter of Jasper Carrott. Born and raised in Solihull
* [Richard Hammond](/wiki/Richard_Hammond "Richard Hammond") – *[Top Gear](/wiki/Top_Gear_(2002_TV_series) "Top Gear (2002 TV series)")* presenter and radio presenter was born in Solihull.
* [Martin Johnson](/wiki/Martin_Johnson_(rugby_union) "Martin Johnson (rugby union)") – [Rugby World Cup](/wiki/Rugby_World_Cup "Rugby World Cup")- winning England team captain and later their manager was born in Solihull.
* [John Wyndham](/wiki/John_Wyndham "John Wyndham") – Science fiction author (*[The Day of the Triffids](/wiki/The_Day_of_the_Triffids "The Day of the Triffids")*) was born in [Dorridge](/wiki/Dorridge "Dorridge") in the Solihull area.

There is a longer list in the article for [Solihull](/wiki/Solihull "Solihull") town.
